column1: # GIAB alpha-globin depth baseline — the raw measurement behind the
# `baseline` column in assets/hba_segments.bed. Committed so the calibration is
# auditable and reproducible rather than a magic number in a script.
# Measured 2026-07-22. Each value is mean depth over the region; *_r columns are
# that depth / the chr2:100000000-100020000 control depth.
#   INTER_A2_A1  chr16:173711-176679   (the -a3.7 diagnostic segment)
# BASELINE = median of HG002-HG007 (n=6):  HBZ 0.760 · HBA2 0.750 · INTER_A2_A1 1.001 · HBA1 0.964
# HG001 IS EXCLUDED. It reads low across all three segments (0.286/0.503/0.548)
# while its control is normal at 30.87. Against the n=6 baseline that is
# 0.38/0.67/0.57, which matches NO allele — a --FIL/--THAI het would give ~0.5
# on all three. Most likely GC dropout in this GC-rich subtelomeric region from
# HG001's library chemistry, but depth alone cannot separate that from a real
# deletion. STATUS UNRESOLVED: do not use HG001 as an alpha normal, and do not
# record it as a carrier either.
# CAVEAT: these six are assumed alpha-normal because nothing says otherwise, not
# because they were typed. -a3.7 is common worldwide and HG005/6/7 are Han
# Chinese, where --SEA and -a3.7 both occur. A silent carrier among them would
# drag the baseline down and make the caller UNDER-call deletions. Orthogonal
# typing (gap-PCR/MLPA) would settle it.
sample
HG001
HG002
HG003
HG004
HG005
HG006
HG007
